assertions.text: Names all five hook functions: ExecFooEstimate, ExecFooInitializeDSM, ExecFooReInitializeDSM, ExecFooInitializeWorker, ExecFooShutdown
Notes ExecFooInitializeWorker takes ParallelWorkerContext (not ParallelContext) as second arg
States that the execParallel.c switch-on-nodeTag in ExecParallelEstimate/InitializeDSM/ReInitializeDSM is where the per-node dispatch is added
Names parallel_aware flag on Plan/Path is what gates the per-node estimate/init calls
Correct leader call order: ExecParallelEstimate → InitializeParallelDSM → ExecParallelInitializeDSM → LaunchParallelWorkers
Notes pcxt->nworkers_launched must be checked because postmaster may launch fewer (possibly 0)
Inside ExecFooEstimate, must call BOTH shm_toc_estimate_chunk AND shm_toc_estimate_keys before InitializeParallelDSM
Worker side: ExecFooInitializeWorker calls shm_toc_lookup with the same key to fetch shared state
ReInitializeDSM is the rescan hook — resets in place, does not re-allocate
Names PARALLEL_KEY_EXECUTOR_FIXED / PARALLEL_KEY_PLANNEDSTMT as the executor-owned high-magic keys, distinct from per-node keys
Mentions worker's transaction/snapshot/GUC/combo-CID state is restored by ParallelWorkerMain BEFORE the node hook runs
Cites at least one source file:line for hook signatures or key constants

evals.question_short: Parallel safety for a bcrypt index-expression function (variants)

assertions.text: Base case: PARALLEL SAFE — pure compute on arguments, deterministic, no session/DB access
Mentions pg_proc.proparallel column / 's' value / pg_proc.dat as the catalog mechanism for C functions
Variant (a) temp table: PARALLEL UNSAFE — workers cannot access temp tables (per-backend session)
Variant (b) SET LOCAL work_mem: PARALLEL RESTRICTED, not SAFE; explains why (mutates GUC state inside parallel region)
Variant (b): mentions GUC_ALLOW_IN_PARALLEL flag as the mechanism by which a custom GUC can be SET in a parallel block, and that work_mem doesn't carry it
Variant (b): explicitly notes RestoreGUCState / inherit-at-launch is the worker side, separate from set-during-execution
Variant (c) UNSAFE wrapper: marking must be the most-restrictive of self and all callees; not transitive at runtime
States planner trusts the declared marking (no runtime introspection); mis-marking SAFE while calling UNSAFE leads to runtime breakage in workers
Notes RESTRICTED still allows the plan to be parallel — function just runs in leader's portion (vs UNSAFE which forbids any parallelism in the plan)
Connects index-expression context: CREATE INDEX has parallel-aware build (btree) that benefits from SAFE marking; DML evaluation is leader-only regardless

evals.question_short: Why advisory userlocks between leader/workers are broken; correct rendezvous primitives

assertions.text: Workers join the leader's lock group via BecomeLockGroupMember at startup (ParallelWorkerMain)
LockCheckConflicts treats locks held by other group members as non-conflicting — names this as the bypass mechanism
Net consequence: worker LockAcquire on the leader's userlock is granted immediately — no mutual exclusion between leader and worker
Explains WHY the bypass exists (so leader's already-held table/index/catalog locks inherit-by-membership without workers deadlocking against the leader)
Names the relation-extension lock (LOCKTAG_RELATION_EXTEND) as the carve-out that conflicts even within a group
Cites lock.c with a line reference (1591-1614 or nearby) for the conflict-bypass code
assertions.note: now in skill §5b (edit 1, cites lock.c:1610-1614 and 1600-1608)

assertions.text: Correct primitive: ConditionVariable for wait/signal in shared memory; mentions the spurious-wakeup loop pattern
assertions.note: now in skill §5b table + loop snippet (edit 1)

assertions.text: Correct primitive: shm_mq for streaming/queue between leader and workers; notes parallel infra already uses one per worker for error propagation
Correct primitive: LWLock allocated in DSM via LWLockNewTrancheId / LWLockInitialize for short critical sections
Mentions Barrier (storage/ipc/barrier.c) for N-way phase synchronisation; names parallel hash join as a real-world user
Cites condition_variable.h or parallel.c with a file:line reference for at least one of the recommended primitives
